title: Faarl
content:

{{Quote|Faarl is, in his own way, as dangerous as any of the factions of the Empire.|General Airen Cracken|<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />}}
'''Faarl''', called '''"Faarl the Conqueror"''' or '''"The Child Conqueror"''', was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]], [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]], and [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] who managed to [[Faarl's empire|conquer]] several low-technology [[Planet/Legends|planets]] in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] before his twentieth [[Birthday/Legends|birthday]].

==Biography==
===Early years===
Allegedly born the eldest son of a noble family in the [[Korlings system/Legends|Korlings system]], Faarl somehow gathered a small group of a few dozen [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] under his command at a young age. At first, his band merely plundered weakly-defended [[Star system/Legends|systems]] and captured poorly-armed [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]]. Soon, however, he discovered the [[Parthovian Cluster/Legends|Parthovian Cluster]], a part of [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] with several inhabited [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] rich in plunder but low in technology. Better still for Faarl's purposes, the [[Star cluster/Legends|star cluster]] was far from the reach of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />

In a few months, his raids added three [[CR90 corvette/Legends|Corellian corvettes]], six [[DP20 gunship/Legends|Corellian gunships]], and a score of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] to his fleet. At the same time, his reputation grew, leading many pirates to join his band. Once his forces were strong enough, he revealed his plan and led his forces to a planet in the Parthovian Cluster. This planet's steam-powered technology was no match for Faarl's modern weapons, allowing him to conquer the planet and enslave its populace within two months. He then made alliances with the planet's natives, giving them positions of power in exchange for a tribute of [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] and other valuables.<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />

[[File:Tulvaree.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Faarl made [[Tulvaree]] into his soldiers.]]

This pattern of conquest was repeated in Faarl's later campaigns, allowing him to increase his forces with by adding [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] and slave soldiers from each new conquest. Each twelve-person unit in his army was commanded by a pirate who had been with him before he began his conquests. Mercenaries made up half of each unit, with the rest being low-ranking slave soldiers. These slaves could, in theory, earn their freedom in combat, but the free mercenaries and pirate officers ensured that this rarely happened by ordering slaves into the most lethal situations. Faarl's mercenary troops were not entirely trustworthy, but the authority they were given over the slave soldiers, the promise of lethal force from the soldiers who would remain loyal in a possible revolt, and most importantly the promise of plunder, staved off any possible mutiny.<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />

His next conquest was the planet [[Pochi]], another primitive planet [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] an unnamed and uncharted [[Star/Legends|sun]]. Faarl named the sun and the system [[Faarlsun]] in his own honor. Finding the ancient [[Fortress Baarlos]] on the planet, he decided to make it his permanent base of operations. He also found that the native [[Sentience/Legends|semi-sentient]] [[Tulvaree]]s made excellent soldiers, and added many of them to his army.<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />

===Empire building===
[[File:Fortress_Baarlos.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Fortress Baarlos]], Faarl's headquarters.]]

Faarl made Pochi his [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world. His forces expanded Fortress Baarlos by excavating catacombs beneath the tower, and adding [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shield]] generators and an [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] for defense. In addition to dungeons for slaves and [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]], and quarters for his soldiers and aides, he organized an extensive library of [[Holovid/Legends|holovids]] and a huge assembly chamber where he held court, received tribute, and viewed entertainment. A connoisseur of literature and art, Faarl hired over one hundred artists and entertainers for his court, including writers, [[Musician/Legends|musicians]], and a troupe of [[Tetranni body-sculptor]]s. He also built a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] on Pochi, which encouraged the growth of the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Vindru]].<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />

Faarl's activities led [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]], the head of [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]], to set a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] of 50,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for Faarl's capture in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] was too busy fighting the Galactic Empire at the time, however, and was unable to take direct action against his remote conquests.<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" /> Faarl was eventually [[Assault on Fortress Baarlos|captured]] by [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], who defeated his Tulvaree guards, entered Fortress Baarlos, and captured the young warlord.<ref name="The History of the Mandalorians">{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
"The Child Conqueror" was described by General Cracken as a "cunning warrior." Though he rarely took the field, preferring to direct his forces from his hidden base, Faarl often wore a suit of [[battle armor]] and armed himself with a [[powerstaff]].<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />
